raw_title: Temporal dynamics of media and communication processes: a review of theories and meta-analysis of empirical studies
raw_abstract: Abstract This study synthesized research from 4 theoretical frameworks that explicitly conceptualize media and communication as dynamic processes over time, emphasizing reciprocal and self-causing effects. A meta-analysis of 223 effect sizes from 103 studies supported the presence of both reciprocal and self-causing relationships, suggesting their robustness as communication phenomena. Time lag moderated self-causing effects, such that longer intervals weakened these effects. Longer lags attenuating the influence of media/communication on subsequent outcomes approached but did not reach statistical significance, and did not significantly alter the reverse pathway. Exploratory moderation analyses showed that effect sizes varied by audience age, analytic methods, topic areas, and media types. Findings highlight the importance of time lag specification in studying dynamic media effects.
title_en: Temporal dynamics of media and communication processes: a review of theories and meta-analysis of empirical studies
title_zh: 媒体与传播过程的时间动态：理论回顾与实证研究的元分析
abstract_en: Abstract This study synthesized research from 4 theoretical frameworks that explicitly conceptualize media and communication as dynamic processes over time, emphasizing reciprocal and self-causing effects. A meta-analysis of 223 effect sizes from 103 studies supported the presence of both reciprocal and self-causing relationships, suggesting their robustness as communication phenomena. Time lag moderated self-causing effects, such that longer intervals weakened these effects. Longer lags attenuating the influence of media/communication on subsequent outcomes approached but did not reach statistical significance, and did not significantly alter the reverse pathway. Exploratory moderation analyses showed that effect sizes varied by audience age, analytic methods, topic areas, and media types. Findings highlight the importance of time lag specification in studying dynamic media effects.
abstract_zh: 摘要 本研究综合了四个明确将媒体和传播概念化为随时间变化的动态过程的理论框架的研究，强调相互和自我因果效应。对103项研究的223个效应量进行的元分析支持了相互和自我因果关系的存在，表明它们作为传播现象的稳健性。时间滞后调节了自我因果效应，较长的间隔削弱了这些效应。较长的滞后减弱媒体/传播对后续结果的影响接近但未达到统计显著性，并且没有显著改变反向路径。探索性调节分析显示，效应量因受众年龄、分析方法、主题领域和媒体类型而异。研究结果强调了在研究动态媒体效应时时间滞后规范的重要性。
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究回顾了四个理论，这些理论认为媒体和传播是随时间变化的动态过程，强调相互影响和自我影响。通过分析103项研究的数据，发现媒体和传播确实存在相互和自我因果效应，这些效应很稳定。研究发现，时间间隔越长，自我影响越弱。时间间隔对媒体影响后续结果的作用接近显著但未完全达到，且不影响反向路径。研究还发现，效应大小受受众年龄、分析方法、主题和媒体类型影响。总之，研究强调在研究媒体如何随时间影响人们时，考虑时间间隔很重要。
plain_summary_en: This study reviewed four theories that view media and communication as dynamic processes over time, focusing on how they influence each other and themselves. By analyzing data from 103 studies, it found that media and communication do have mutual and self-causing effects, which are quite stable. The study showed that longer time intervals weaken self-influence. Time intervals had a near-significant but not fully significant effect on how media influence later outcomes, and did not affect the reverse path. It also found that effect sizes vary based on audience age, analysis methods, topics, and media types. In short, the research highlights the importance of considering time intervals when studying how media affects people over time.
research_direction_zh: 本研究属于传播学中的媒体效应研究领域，特别关注时间维度在媒体和传播过程中的作用，探索动态、非线性的影响机制，旨在理解媒体如何随时间塑造和反作用于社会行为与认知。
research_direction_en: This study falls within the field of media effects research in communication studies, specifically focusing on the role of time dimensions in media and communication processes. It explores dynamic, non-linear influence mechanisms to understand how media shapes and reacts to social behaviors and cognitions over time.
